title: Cascaded multi-task expert for stop now, laps-to-stop, and next compound
summary: Recent F1 pit-stop work uses a cascaded multi-task design where next-lap stop probability is complemented by a regression head for remaining laps, and older race-strategy work explicitly splits pit/no-pit from next-compound choice.
rationale: Recent F1 pit-stop work uses a cascaded multi-task design where next-lap stop probability is complemented by a regression head for remaining laps, and older race-strategy work explicitly splits pit/no-pit from next-compound choice. That structure matches this problem better than a single scalar target because pit timing and intended tyre choice are coupled.
implementation_hint: Create auxiliary labels from future rows: clipped laps-until-next-pit and next compound after the next pit. Train a joint model or cascade, then feed OOF auxiliary predictions into the main GBM/CatBoost. For races involving inters/wets, allow a separate auxiliary regime or class grouping.
expected_effect: Sharper localization of true pit windows and better handling of compound-rule logic than a single binary head.
risk: Future-derived auxiliary labels can be noisy near race end, red-flag-like resets, or missing follow-up laps; added complexity can wash out if the auxiliary targets are poorly learned.
